title: Garhwa Fort
tagline: Garhwa Fort — heritage city of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Garhwa fort is a medieval fortified enclosure, housing a temple complex in Prayagraj district, Uttar Pradesh, India, belonging to the Gupta Period.
overview.description: Garhwa fort is a medieval fortified enclosure, housing a temple complex in Prayagraj district, Uttar Pradesh, India, belonging to the Gupta Period. It is most known for its Gadhwa Stone Inscriptions early 5th-century CE Sanskrit inscriptions by Chandragupta II and Kumaragupta. The fort is situated 50 km south-west of Prayagraj, on the Jabalpur road, 5 km from Shankargarh. The ancient name of Garhwa was Bhattpraya; in fact, there is a village called Bhattgarh nearby, which is currently named Bargarh.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Gadhwa Stone Inscriptions
topPlaces.description: The Gadhwa Stone Inscriptions, or Garhwa Stone Inscriptions, are early 5th-century CE Sanskrit inscriptions discovered at Garhwa Fort, Uttar Pradesh relating to a series of charitable donations to various sattra (almshouses) by Gupta Empire rulers Chandragupta II and Kumaragupta I. The inscription is notable for including symbols for numerals "8, 10, 80 and 90" in the 5th-century, as well as mentioning the ancient city of Pataliputra (modern Patna).

topPlaces.name: Shankargarh
topPlaces.description: Shankargarh is a town and a nagar panchayat in Prayagraj district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is also known as Kasauta Shankargarh.

topPlaces.name: Bara Thermal Power Station
topPlaces.description: Prayagraj Power Generation Company Limited is a coal-based, 3×660 MW, super critical thermal power plant located in Bara Tehsil in Prayagraj district, Uttar Pradesh. The power plant is owned by Renascent Power, a subsidiary of the Tata Power.

topPlaces.name: Prayag Sangeet Samiti
topPlaces.description: Prayag Sangeet Samiti is an institute imparting education in Hindustani Classical music, in Prayagraj, India. It awards diplomas and certificates in vocal music, instrumental music as well as classical dance.

items.note: The Gadhwa Stone Inscriptions, or Garhwa Stone Inscriptions, are early 5th-century CE Sanskrit inscriptions discovered at Garhwa Fort, Uttar Pradesh relating to a series of charitable donations to various sattra (almshouses) by Gupta Empire rulers Chandragupta II and Kumaragupta I. The inscription is notable for including symbols for numerals "8, 10, 80 and 90" in the 5th-century, as well as mentioning the ancient city of Pataliputra (modern Patna).
Shankargarh is a town and a nagar panchayat in Prayagraj district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is also known as Kasauta Shankargarh.

Prayag Sangeet Samiti is an institute imparting education in Hindustani Classical music, in Prayagraj, India. It awards diplomas and certificates in vocal music, instrumental music as well as classical dance.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Garhwa Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Garhwa Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Garhwa Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Prayagraj Airport (Bamrauli) (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Prayagraj Junction (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Garhwa Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Garhwa Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Garhwa Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Garhwa Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Garhwa Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gadhwa Stone Inscriptions, Shankargarh, Bara Thermal Power Station, Prayag Sangeet Samiti.
